name: rubocop
description: A Ruby static code analyzer and formatter, based on the community Ruby style guide

name: -l
--lint
options.description: Run only lint cops

name: -x
--fix-layout
options.description: Run only layout cops, with autocorrect on

options.name: --safe
options.description: Run only safe cops

options.name: --except
options.description: Exclude the given cop(s)

options.name: --only
options.description: Run only the given cop(s)

options.name: --only-guide-cops
options.description: Run only cops for rules that link to a style guide

name: -F
--fail-fast
options.description: Inspect files in order of modification time and stop after the first file containing offenses

options.name: --disable-pending-cops
options.description: Run without pending cops

options.name: --enable-pending-cops
options.description: Run with pending cops

options.name: --ignore-disable-comments
options.description: Run cops even when they are disabled locally by a `rubocop:disable` directive

options.name: --force-exclusion
options.description: Any files excluded by `Exclude` in configuration files will be excluded, even if given explicitly as arguments

options.name: --only-recognized-file-types
options.description: Inspect files given on the command line only if they are listed in `AllCops/Include` parameters of user configuration or default configuration

options.name: --ignore-parent-exclusion
options.description: Prevent from inheriting `AllCops/Exclude` from parent folders

options.name: --ignore-unrecognized-cops
options.description: Ignore unrecognized cops or departments in the config

options.name: --force-default-config
options.description: Use default configuration even if configuration files are present in the directory tree

name: --stdin
-s
options.description: Pipe source from STDIN, using FILE in offense reports. This is useful for editor integration

name: -P
--parallel
options.description: Use available CPUs to execute inspection in parallel default true

options.name: --no-parallel
options.description: Disable parallel inspection (default: false)

options.name: --fail-level
options.description: Minimum severity for exit with error code

name: -C
--cache
options.description: Use result caching (FLAG=true) or don't (FLAG=false), default determined by configuration parameter AllCops: UseCache

options.name: --cache-root
options.description: Set the cache root directory. Takes precedence over the configuration parameter AllCops: CacheRootDirectory and the $RUBOCOP_CACHE_ROOT environment variable

options.name: --no-server
options.description: Do not use server even if it's available

options.name: --server
options.description: If a server process has not been started yet, start the server process and execute inspection with server.  Default is true.  You can specify the server host and port with the $RUBOCOP_SERVER_HOST and the $RUBOCOP_SERVER_PORT environment variables

options.name: --restart-server
options.description: Restart server process

options.name: --start-server
options.description: Start server process

options.name: --stop-server
options.description: Stop server process

options.name: --server-status
options.description: Show server status

name: -f
--format
options.description: Choose an output formatter. This option can be specified multiple times to enable multiple formatters at the same time

name: -D
--display-cop-names
options.description: Display cop names in offense messages.  Default is true

options.name: --no-display-cop-names
options.description: Disable displaying cop names in offense messages. Default false

name: -E
--extra-details
options.description: Display extra details in offense messages

name: -S
--display-style-guide
options.description: Display style guide URLs in offense messages

name: -o
--out
options.description: Write output to a file instead of STDOUT.  This option applies to the previously specified --format, or the default format if no format is specified

options.name: --stderr
options.description: Write all output to stderr except for the autocorrected source. This is especially useful when combined with --autocorrect and --stdin

options.name: --display-time
options.description: Display elapsed time in seconds

options.name: --display-only-failed
options.description: Only output offense messages. Omit passing cops. Only valid for --format junit

options.name: --display-only-fail-level-offenses
options.description: Only output offense messages at the specified --fail-level or above

options.name: --display-only-correctable
options.description: Only output correctable offense messages

options.name: --display-only-safe-correctable
options.description: Only output safe-correctable offense messages when combined with --display-only-correctable

name: -a
--autocorrect
options.description: Autocorrect offenses (only when it's safe)

name: -A
--autocorrect-all
options.description: Autocorrect offenses (safe and unsafe)

options.name: --disable-uncorrectable
options.description: Annotates any offenses that do not support autocorrect with `rubocop:todo` comments

options.name: --auto-gen-config
options.description: Generate a configuration file acting as a TODO list

options.name: --regenerate-todo
options.description: Regenerate the TODO configuration file using the last configuration. If there is no existing TODO file, acts like --auto-gen-config

options.name: --exclude-limit
options.description: Set the limit for how many files to explicitly exclude.  If there are more files than the limit, the cop will be disabled instead. Default is 15

options.name: --offense-counts
options.description: Include offense counts in configuration file generated by --auto-gen-config.  Default is true

options.name: --auto-gen-only-exclude
options.description: Generate only Exclude parameters and not Max when running --auto-gen-config, except if the number of files with offenses is bigger than exclude-limit. Default is false

options.name: --auto-gen-timestamp
options.description: Include the date and time when the --auto-gen-config was run in the file it generates. Default is true

name: -L
--list-target-files
options.description: List all files RuboCop will inspect

options.name: --show-cops
options.description: Shows the given cops, or all cops by default, and their configurations for the current directory

options.name: --show-docs-url
options.description: Display url to documentation for the given cops, or base url by default

options.name: --init
options.description: Generate a .rubocop.yml file in the current directory

name: -c
--config
options.description: Specify configuration file

name: -d
--debug
options.description: Display debug info

name: -r
--require
options.description: Require Ruby file

options.name: --no-color
options.description: Disable color output

options.name: --color
options.description: Force color output

name: -v
--version
options.description: Display version

name: -V
--verbose-version
options.description: Display verbose version
